name: 90s Pop Culture
description: Music, hits, and cultural moments from the 1990s

questions.q: The Rembrandts' 'I'll Be There for You' was the theme song for which 1990s sitcom?

source.notes: Debuted September 22, 1994. Ran for 10 seasons.

questions.q: Which Manchester rock band released the iconic track 'Wonderwall' in 1995?

source.notes: Written by Noel Gallagher. From the album '(What's the Story) Morning Glory?'

questions.q: What was the Spice Girls' debut single, released in 1996?

source.notes: Hit #1 in 37 countries. Written in 30 minutes.

questions.q: In the Spice Girls, Mel B was known by which 'Spice' nickname?

source.notes: Nicknames coined by Top of the Pops magazine in 1996.

questions.q: Which Irish rock band had a 1994 hit with the politically charged song 'Zombie'?

source.notes: Written by Dolores O'Riordan. Protested the 1993 Warrington bombings.

questions.q: Which Danish-Norwegian group had a massive 1997 hit with 'Barbie Girl'?

source.notes: Mattel sued (unsuccessfully) over the song.

questions.q: TLC's 1999 hit 'No Scrubs' is about refusing to date men who lack what?

source.notes: From 'FanMail' album. Grammy-nominated.

questions.q: Which band's 1997 single 'Bittersweet Symphony' famously sampled a Rolling Stones orchestral recording?

source.notes: Songwriting credit was given to Jagger/Richards. Returned to The Verve in 2019.

questions.q: Cher's 1998 hit 'Believe' was one of the first pop songs to prominently use which vocal effect?

source.notes: The 'Cher effect.' Used pitch correction as a creative tool, not just a fix.

questions.q: Which band released 'Don't Speak' in 1996, featuring lead singer Gwen Stefani?

source.notes: From 'Tragic Kingdom.' Written about Stefani's breakup with bandmate Tony Kanal.

questions.q: Radiohead's early hit 'Creep,' released in 1992, comes from which debut album?

source.notes: Pablo Honey released February 1993. 'Creep' initially flopped in the UK.

questions.q: House of Pain's 1992 party anthem 'Jump Around' is associated with which musical genre?

source.notes: Everlast, Danny Boy, DJ Lethal. Still a staple at sporting events.

questions.q: Which French electronic duo released 'Around the World' in 1997?

source.notes: Guy-Manuel and Thomas Bangalter. From the album 'Homework.'

questions.q: On 'Saturday Night Live' in 1992, which Irish singer caused controversy by tearing up a photo of the Pope?

source.notes: October 3, 1992. Protesting child abuse in the Catholic Church.

questions.q: In 1993, which artist legally changed his name to an unpronounceable symbol during a dispute with his record label?

source.notes: Protesting Warner Bros. contract. Known as 'The Artist Formerly Known as Prince' until 2000.

questions.q: Ricky Martin got everyone dancing in 1999 with which Latin pop hit?

source.notes: Hit #1 in over 20 countries. Helped launch the 'Latin Explosion.'

questions.q: Boyz II Men spent a record-breaking 14 weeks at #1 in 1992 with which ballad?

source.notes: From the 'Boomerang' soundtrack. Produced by Babyface.

questions.q: Whitney Houston's massive 1992 hit 'I Will Always Love You' was originally written and recorded by which country artist?

source.notes: Parton wrote it in 1973 about parting from Porter Wagoner. Houston's version was for 'The Bodyguard.'

questions.q: In 1999, Britney Spears released her debut single. What was it called?

source.notes: Written by Max Martin. Music video set in a school.

questions.q: Hanson's 1997 hit 'MMMBop' was performed by three brothers. What were their first names?

source.notes: From Tulsa, Oklahoma. Taylor sang lead at age 14.
